name: Nasarre and San Urbez Loop
name_original: San Urbez y Nasarre – circular entre las sierras de Guara y Aineto
description: This difficult 21.2-mile mountain bike route in Sierra de Guara explores abandoned villages and offers challenging terrain.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Nueno are situated in the province of Huesca, Aragon, Spain, at the foot of the Sierra de Gratal and near the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. The region features dramatic limestone cliffs, deep canyons carved by rivers, and diverse ecosystems ranging from dense woodlands to rocky sections. This varied terrain provides a network of mountain bike routes through the Hoya de Huesca, characterized by unique aggregate rock formations and spectacular ravines. Best mountain bike trails around Nueno The most popular mountain bike trail is Vuelta a Gabardiella desde Santa María de Belsué por Nocito, a 22.5 miles (36.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers challenging climbs and panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Nasarre and San Urbez Loop, a difficult 21.2 miles (34.1 km) path. This route traverses varied landscapes, including historical sites and natural features. Local mountain bikers also love the San Urbez Sanctuary and Ibirque Dolmen Loop, a 13.4 miles (21.5 km) trail leading through diverse terrain with cultural landmarks, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Mountain biking around Nueno is defined by dramatic canyons, limestone cliffs, and diverse woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Nueno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 280 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Nueno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Nueno?
A: Nueno offers a diverse network of nearly 30 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy paths to challenging, difficult trails.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Nueno?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail around Nueno. While many routes are moderate to difficult, beginners can find suitable options to enjoy the region's landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Nueno?
A: The terrain around Nueno is incredibly varied, situated at the foot of the Sierra de Gratal and near the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. You can expect dramatic limestone cliffs, deep canyons carved by rivers, and diverse ecosystems ranging from dense woodlands to rocky sections. The Hoya de Huesca area, where Nueno is located, features unique aggregate rock formations and spectacular ravines, offering a mix of challenging climbs and technical descents.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging mountain bike routes in Nueno?
A: Yes, Nueno is home to several challenging mountain bike routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977114020/vuelta-a-gabardiella-desde-santa-maria-de-belsue-por-nocito" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta a Gabardiella desde Santa María de Belsué por Nocito&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.5-mile (36.1 km) trail with significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976410910/nasarre-and-san-urbez-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nasarre and San Urbez Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 21.2-mile (34.1 km) path traversing varied landscapes.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Nueno?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails around Nueno are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976399840/san-urbez-sanctuary-and-ibirque-dolmen-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Urbez Sanctuary and Ibirque Dolmen Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.4-mile (21.5 km) route that takes you through diverse terrain and past cultural landmarks.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails in Nueno?
A: The trails often lead through or near stunning natural features. You might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5040942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgas de San Julián&lt;/a&gt;, a dramatic gorge, or ride past the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2981345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its dramatic canyons, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorge of the Río Alcanadre&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic Isuela River Valley.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore while mountain biking in Nueno?
A: Yes, the area around Nueno is rich in history. You can find ancient sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Ibirque&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2984930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Losa Mora&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33790209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Ibirque – Nocito Village loop from Nocito&lt;/a&gt;, specifically incorporate these historical points of interest into the ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Nueno?
A: The mountain bike trails around Nueno are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the dramatic canyons, limestone cliffs, and diverse woodlands that define the region's unique riding experience. The variety of routes, from moderate paths to more challenging options, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Nueno?
A: Nueno's location offers a mix of Mediterranean and Atlantic climates. While specific weather conditions vary, spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential snow in higher elevations during winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Nueno?
A: Absolutely. The region's dramatic geological formations, including the Sierra de Gratal and the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, offer numerous opportunities for breathtaking views. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10465639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto Roldán Viewpoint – Apiés loop from Sabayés&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to take you to prominent viewpoints, allowing you to admire the imposing rock walls and expansive landscapes.
tips:
Nasarre is a small uninhabited town located on a hillock halfway between the towns of Bara and Otín, on the Mascún and Alcanadre watershed. Its buildings stand on a plain a few meters east of the Picarizas ravine.

More information: http://www.despobladosenhuesca.com/2011/04/nasarre.html#:~:text=Nasarre%20es%20un%20peque%C3%B1o%20pueblo,del%20barranco%20de%20las%20Picarizas.

A beautiful abandoned monastery with incredible views and a very interesting story:

The monastery must have been founded at the beginning of the 8th century, before the Muslim invasion. In the seventeenth century it was renovated and expanded transforming the previous Romanesque church. The current sanctuary is formed by the church with a Latin cross plan with three naves. The central and lateral nave are covered by vaults of lunettes. The tower, low in height, stands next to the access. In front of the sanctuary stands the main door, of Renaissance style, carried out in 1701. It is made up of a huge atrium with open access on three sides by means of semicircular arches. On one side there is a small square where the most picturesque façade stands, in front of a viewpoint with an unbeatable view of the Tozal de Guara.

The life of San Úrbez was agitated. He was born in Bordeaux around the year 702 and moved to settle as a hermit and shepherd in Alto Aragón. He took the habits in San Martín de la Val de Onsera, in whose humble monastery he intended to retire. But after a long stay there, the bishop of Huesca ordained him a priest and sent him to Nocito as a priest. He was fulfilling his mission but living on the outskirts, in a cave near the monastery. He died in 802, when he was 100 years old. The relic of San Úrbez deposited in this sanctuary was the most complete since it had the mummified body of the saint in which his beard and hair could be appreciated. Here the relics were deposited until in 1936 they were thrown into a bonfire during the civil war.

More information: https://www.joreate.com/tag/santuario-san-urbez/

Architectural information: http://www.sipca.es/censo/1-INM-HUE-006-163-011/Santuario/de/San/%C3%9Arbez.html#.YDThMJNKjUI

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult for mountain biking. The Sierra de Guara region, where it's located, is known for its challenging and diverse terrain, including steep climbs, descents, and potentially loose gravel sections that require good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the San Urbez y Nasarre circular route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse and challenging terrain. The route traverses limestone massifs, potentially deep canyons, and forested areas. Some sections may feature steep climbs on forest tracks and dynamic descents. Be prepared for varied surfaces, including loose, thick gravel in places.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through some remarkable spots. You'll pass by Nasarre, a charming abandoned village, and the Beautiful Abandoned Sanctuary of San Urbez, which is built into a cave. The area also offers impressive scenery, including views of canyons and cliffs.

faqs.question: Is the San Urbez y Nasarre route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating and the challenging terrain typical of the Sierra de Guara, this route is generally not recommended for beginners. It requires a good level of physical fitness and mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Sendero Histórico: Huesca (36%), Etapa 4: Laguarta - Nocito (1%), Camino de Abellada (2%), and Camino de Used a Santa Cilia (1%).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike in the Sierra de Guara region, and thus this route, is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). The weather is generally milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential snow or colder conditions in winter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the San Urbez y Nasarre mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking for this combined route isn't detailed, access points for trails in the Sierra de Guara often involve parking in nearby villages like Nocito or at designated trailheads. It's advisable to check local signage or community forums for the most current parking information near the start of your chosen segment.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: As this route is within the Parque Natural de la Sierra y los Cañones de Guara, dogs are generally allowed but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always follow local regulations and clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route in the Parque Natural de la Sierra y los Cañones de Guara?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific entrance fees for accessing the trails within the Parque Natural de la Sierra y los Cañones de Guara. However, certain activities or specific areas might require permits, especially for canyoning or large groups. For mountain biking, standard trail use typically does not require a permit, but it's always good practice to check the official park website for any updated regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter along the route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Guara is rich in biodiversity. Keep an eye out for various bird species, including raptors like the majestic bearded vulture (quebrantahuesos), which is known to inhabit the region's cliffs and canyons. You may also spot other local fauna and diverse flora, including beeches, willows, and pines.

faqs.question: What should I bring for mountain biking this difficult route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and the challenging terrain, you should bring appropriate mountain biking gear, including a well-maintained bike, helmet, gloves, and protective padding. Essential items include plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit, first-aid supplies, navigation tools (GPS or map), and suitable clothing for varying weather conditions. Sun protection is also highly recommended.

waypoints.name: Beautiful Abandoned Sanctuary of San Urbez
